summary: Set 093 Session 1: every Work Explorer module row now renders two PERSISTENT semantic child nodes — Plan (present/missing) and Session sets (blocked-until-plan / empty / bucketed, with the status buckets nested UNDER Session sets, never replacing the checklist). ARIA levels shifted to module 1 / Plan & Session sets 2 / bucket 3 / row 4 (aria-setsize=2 on the fixed pair; aria-expanded on Session sets only when bucketed); keyboard nav and collapse reach the new nodes via the existing shared toggler. States derive from the pure deriveModuleChildren(planPresent, setCount); planExists is resolved host-side per root (guarded against a null planPath) and ORed across worktree roots in mergeVisibleModules, with the null guard also enforced in the pure buildVisibleModulePayloads so a fallback (planPath null) can never read present. A routed architecture ruling (opus, tier 3) settled that all three module kinds render both children with no exemption. Layer 2 (deriveModuleChildren matrix, per-kind payload, merge-OR) and Layer 3 (session-sets-tree + module-tier) pins moved with the new level (amendment 6). Suite green: pytest 2922/6 skipped, extension unit 1419, Playwright 23, tsc clean. No Marketplace publish (single release boundary after Set 094).

reason.specifics: Session 2 (row action strip, QuickPick retirement, assign-legacy flow) builds directly on Session 1's child-node model and touches the same host/webview/protocol surfaces; the routed analysis (gemini-2.5-pro, s1-assignment-analysis.json) recommends the same Opus seat at medium effort because the warm architectural context sharply lowers the cost of the cross-cutting pickModuleForAuthoring targeting-seam refactor and the set-terminal path-aware critique.
